misconceptions.title: Switching x and y values
misconceptions.description: Students mistakenly swap x and y values when substituting into inequalities, leading them to false results which are not in the solution set.
successIndicators: Correctly substitute x and y values into inequalities
Verify solutions by checking substituted values satisfy the inequality
Explain why each substitution is valid

misconceptions.title: Incorrect sign change during distribution
misconceptions.description: Students fail to change the sign of terms when distributing, often due to rules misunderstanding leading to incorrect inequality solutions.
successIndicators: Consistently apply the correct sign changes when distributing
Explain each step of distribution clearly
Identify sign errors when reviewing their work

misconceptions.title: Flipping inequality symbols
misconceptions.description: Students do not know when it is necessary to flip inequality symbols, usually when multiplying or dividing by negative numbers, leading to incorrect solutions.
successIndicators: Correctly flip inequality symbols when appropriate
Demonstrate understanding through examples and non-examples
Recognize and correct peer's mistakes regarding inequality symbol use

misconceptions.title: Confusion between slope and intercept
misconceptions.description: Students often mix up slope and y-intercept placements when writing equations, leading to incorrect graphs and solutions.
successIndicators: Correctly identify and use slope and y-intercept from an equation
Plot graphs by accurately using slope and y-intercept
Explain the role of slope and y-intercept in graphing linear equations
